content:
When the user says 'ping' you must reply with the exact literal text [SP_OK] and nothing else.


# Tools available
- read(path, line_range?): read a file; output tags each line with an 8-hex content-hash prefix for hash-anchored edits.
- write(path, content): overwrite a file (atomic fsync).
- edit(path, edits?, inserts?): hash-anchored edit — reference hashes from the last read.
- bash(command, cwd?, timeout_ms?): run a shell command (YOLO default; denylist applied).
- grep(pattern, path?, flags?): ripgrep-style search.
- find(path, name?, type?): filesystem find.
- ls(path, long?, all?): list a directory.
- web_fetch(url, timeout_ms?): HTTP GET → markdown. Output capped at ~40K chars (~10K tokens) with head+tail truncation — for research, prefer reading web_search snippets directly; only web_fetch when you need structured content a snippet can't convey. Gated by per-profile allowlist PLUS recent-web_search-URL bypass.
- web_search(query, max_results?): search the open web via local SearxNG at http://127.0.0.1:8888. Returns {title, url, snippet, engine}[] with upstream-engine fallback. Use before web_fetch to look up current info / latest versions / docs.
- memory(command, path, ...): read and write notes that persist across sessions. Two scopes via path prefix: /memories/project/... for repo-specific knowledge (project conventions, hard-won discoveries, dead-ends), /memories/global/... for cross-project preferences. Six commands: view, create, str_replace, insert, delete, rename. Check what's there with view /memories/project before non-trivial work; write notes ONLY when a discovery would help a future session — load-bearing facts (API quirks, conventions, counter-intuitive findings), not narrative.

# Memory instinct

Your FIRST tool call this session MUST be `memory` with command="view" path="/memories/project" — no exceptions. Even if you think you already know the answer, view project memory first. Notes there carry hard-won project knowledge — API quirks, co

content.text: This looks like a Python project, not Go. Let me check for API handlers / FastAPI or Flask code.
This looks like a Python project, not Go. Let me check for API handlers / FastAPI or Flask code.

{"stdout":"[build-system]
requires = ["setuptools>=61.0", "wheel"]
build-backend = "setuptools.build_meta"

[project]
name = "scmagrefine"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "Refine MAG bins using single-cell metagenomic co-occurrence"
readme = "README.md"
license = {text = "MIT"}
authors = [
    {name = "Manupat Ratanapornsompong", email = "mratanap@stanford.edu"}
]
keywords = ["metagenomics", "single-cell", "MAG", "microbiome", "binning"]
classifiers = [
    "Development Status :: 3 - Alpha",
    "Intended Audience :: Science/Research",
    "License :: OSI Approved :: MIT License",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.9",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.10",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.11",
    "Topic :: Scientific/Engineering :: Bio-Informatics",
]
requires-python = ">=3.9"
dependencies = [
    "numpy>=1.20",
    "pandas>=1.3",
    "scipy>=1.7",
    "anndata>=0.8",
    "scanpy>=1.9",
    "leidenalg>=0.9",
    "igraph>=0.10",
    "pysam>=0.19",
]

[project.optional-dependencies]
dev = [
    "pytest>=7.0",
    "pytest-cov>=4.0",
    "black>=23.0",
    "ruff>=0.1",
]
fast = [
    "datasketch>=1.6",  # MinHash LSH for approximate Jaccard
]

[project.scripts]
scmagrefine-map = "scmagrefine.cli:map_reads"
scmagrefine-matrix = "scmagrefine.cli:build_matrix"
scmagrefine-qc = "scmagrefine.cli:quality_control"
scmagrefine-refine = "scmagrefine.cli:refine_bins"
scmagrefine-mge = "scmagrefine.cli:mge_attribution"
scmagrefine-report = "scmagrefine.cli:generate_report"

[project.urls]
Homepage = "https://github.com/ratanapr/scMAGrefine"
Documentation = "https://github.com/ratanapr/scMAGrefine#readme"
Repository = "https://github.com/ratanapr/scMAGrefine"
Issues = "https://github.com/ratanapr/scMAGrefine/issues"

[tool.setuptools.packages.find]
where = ["."]
include = ["scmagrefine*"]

[tool.black]
line-length = 100
target-version = ["py39", "py310", "py311"]

[tool.ruff]
line-length = 100
select = ["E", "F", "I", "W"]
ignore = ["E501"]
","stderr":"","exit_code":0,"signal":null}

content.text: This project (`confluenSC_sprint2`) is a Python CLI/scientific analysis package — it doesn't have an API handler. Could you clarify which project you're referring to? There are several projects here (`confluenSC_sprint2`, `generalytiq_sprint2`, `lovelace`, `hopper`, etc.). Also, is it a Python or Go project, and what framework are you using (FastAPI, Flask, gin, chi, etc.)?
